title: Oden’s Past Begins to Define Wano’s Tragedy
description: On pages 10–13, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Kinemon, Zoro, Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12 as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Give me back my daughter!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I am respon- Bible for this!!

dialogue.speaker: Kinemon
dialogue.text: Kozuki Oden... that cursed child!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: He caused this!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: He is behind another disaster!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: "I was craving some roasted boar...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Give me back my mother!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: How could you do something so cruel!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: No...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Oden two- sword style...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Tougen...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Shira- Tak!!!!*

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Impress- SIVE...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: We should head back.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It is said that an entire village worth of people...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: was eaten by the boar...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The capital may have survived, but nearly a quarter of the city was lost.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Miraculously, there were no human fatalities.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: KIN-CHAN, WAARAM!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Remember between

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: What often did was so cool...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: We should probably thank him, but...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was his fault in the first place! He is a criminal!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He was so cool... I almost cried out in awe!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Everyone Listen!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: I HAVE A CONFESSION TO MAKE....!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: That would be pointless!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I apologise for the timing of this, but...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Waha haha!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: I see, so I have been disowned...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: not only that, but I have also been EXVLED from the capital!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How pathetic, Lord Shogun!! It looks like you...

dialogue.speaker: Wano Citizens
dialogue.text: No, the entire flower capital has admitted...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: that it cannot handle me anymore!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: This is exactly what I wanted!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: time to head to the other side of the river!! I will be sleeping there by sunset!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Ah... I need to eat some- thing...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: By age 18, open had caused the mountain God incident.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 38 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
